name: Sage X3 ERP API
what_it_does: Sage X3 REST API for upper-mid-market and enterprise ERP covering manufacturing, distribution, and financial management. Enables AI agents to manage production order and manufacturing execution for discrete and process manufacturing, handle inventory and multi-warehouse management for distribution operations, access customer order management and delivery scheduling for order fulfillment, retrieve bill of materials and production routing for manufacturing engineering, manage accounts payable and receivable for financial operations, access quality control and regulatory compliance tracking for food, chemical, and pharmaceutical manufacturing, retrieve multi-company and multi-currency financial consolidation for international operations, manage purchasing and supplier contract management for procurement, handle traceability and lot tracking for regulated manufacturing, and integrate Sage X3 data with CRM, MES, and supply chain platforms.
use_cases: Managing production orders from manufacturing execution agents
Tracking lot and batch traceability from food/pharma compliance agents
Processing customer orders from international distribution agents
Consolidating multi-entity financials from accounting agents
not_for: Small businesses without upper-mid-market ERP requirements
Pure services companies without manufacturing or distribution focus
Heavily Salesforce-dependent environments without Sage X3 deployment
best_when: An upper-mid-market manufacturer or distributor in food, chemical, pharma, or industrial sectors using Sage X3 wants AI agents to automate production, inventory, quality, and financial management across multi-site, multi-entity operations.
avoid_when: COMPLIANCE RISK: Sage X3 process manufacturing includes FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and HACCP compliance features; automated recipe management and lot release without quality review creates regulatory exposure for food and pharmaceutical manufacturers. Sage X3 Web Services API (SOAP) is legacy; REST API (introduced in v11/v12) has incomplete coverage — some X3 functionality only accessible via X3 development scripts. Multi-site inventory automation requires careful site context management.

auth.notes: Sage X3 REST API uses OAuth 2.0 (v11+) and basic authentication (older deployments). Developer portal at developer.sage.com. Webhooks for business events. REST API and legacy SOAP Web Services. Newcastle upon Tyne, UK HQ (Sage Group). Public (SGE.L LSE). Sage X3 originally Adonix X3 (acquired 2003). 3,000+ customers globally. Strong in food & beverage, chemicals, pharmaceuticals, and industrial manufacturing. Competes with Epicor, IFS, and Infor for upper-mid-market manufacturing ERP.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual subscription plus implementation
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription per user; implementation and customization fees
pricing.notes: Newcastle, UK. Sage Group plc (SGE.L). Sage X3 formerly Adonix (acquired 2003). 3,000+ global customers. Strong in food & beverage, chemical, and pharmaceutical manufacturing. Competes with Epicor, IFS, and Infor CloudSuite Industrial for upper-mid-market manufacturing ERP.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per deployment; documented per client

known_agent_gotchas: COMPLIANCE RISK: Process manufacturing lot release automation without quality review creates FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and HACCP regulatory exposure for food/pharma manufacturers
REST API version dependency — Sage X3 REST API (v11/v12+) is incomplete; older X3 versions use SOAP Web Services; verify API type for customer's X3 version before integration
X3 development script complexity — advanced Sage X3 customization requires proprietary 4GL development language; complex business logic may not be available via standard API
Multi-site context management — X3 multi-site operations require site/folder context in API calls; missing site context returns wrong inventory and production data
Lot traceability automation — regulated manufacturers require full traceability chain; automated lot creation without complete traceability documentation creates compliance gaps
Recipe and formula management — food/pharma process manufacturing formulas require approval workflow; automated recipe modification without workflow creates regulatory non-compliance
agent_readiness.security_notes: Upper-mid-market ERP. SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2. US/EU. Manufacturing, quality, and financial data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Manufacturing ERP. SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2. US/EU. Manufacturing and financial data.
